1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

25	    @classmethod

27	        return cls(
28	            id=data["id"],
29	            url=data["url"],
30	            title=data["title"],
31	            tags=data.get("tags", []),
32	            notes=data.get("notes", ""),

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

4	from .models import Bookmark

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")

11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:

23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)

27	    for b in bookmarks:

29	        title = b.title[:title_w]

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Python CLI bookmark manager codebase. Here's a complete analysis:

### Project Structure

```
.

├── src/

│   └── test_search.py            (Search tests - 180 lines)
├── .git/                         (Git repo)
└── .gitignore
```

### Project Configuration (pyproject.toml)

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "bookmark-cli"
version = "1.0.0"

test = ["pytest>=7.4.0"]

[project.scripts]
bookmark = "src.cli:main"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
```

**Key points:**
- pytest configuration in pyproject.toml (testpaths = ["tests"])
- No tox.ini, pytest.ini, or setup.cfg files exist
- No requirements.txt (only pyproject.toml)
- Test dependency: pytest>=7.4.0

### Test Files Overview

#### 1. **test_storage.py** (117 lines)
Complete test file for storage functionality. Tests use:
- **Test Framework:** pytest with fixtures
- **Key Fixtures:**
  - `db_path` - creates temp file path using `tmp_path` fixture
  - `sample_bookmarks` - returns 2 Bookmark objects with test data
  
- **Test Classes & Methods:**
  - `TestLoadBookmarks`: 2 tests (empty file, load from file)

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Directory Layout:**

**Entry Point:** The CLI is registered in `pyproject.toml` as:

```
[project.scripts]
bookmark = "src.cli:main"
```

This means running `bookmark` command executes the `main()` function from `src.cli`.

---

### 2. CLI STRUCTURE & FRAMEWORK

**Framework:** **argparse** (standard library, not Click or Typer)

**Main CLI Builder:** `build_parser()` function in `/absolute/path/src/cli.py`

**Command Routing:** Uses `subparsers.add_subparsers(dest="command")` to dispatch to different handlers

**Current Commands Implemented:**

| Command | Handler | Args | Notes |
|---------|---------|------|-------|
| `add` | `add_bookmark()` | `url`, `title`, `--tags`, `--notes` | Creates new bookmark with UUID |
| `list` | `load_bookmarks()` | `--format` (table\|json) | Lists all bookmarks |
| `delete` | `delete_bookmark()` | `id` | Removes bookmark by ID |
| `show` | `get_bookmark()` | `id` | Displays single bookmark |

**Entry Point Flow (src/cli.py:44-81):**
1. Parse arguments using `build_parser()`
2. Check if command exists (exit with help if not)
3. Route to appropriate handler based on `args.command`
4. Print results or error messages

---

### 3. EXISTING COMMANDS - DETAILED IMPLEMENTATION

#### Command: `add`
**Location:** `src/cli.py:52-59`
```python
if args.command == "add":
    bookmark = add_bookmark(
        url=args.url,
        title=args.title,
        tags=args.tags,
        notes=args.notes,
    )
    print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
```
**Arguments:**
- `url` (required) - URL to bookmark
- `title` (required) - Bookmark title
- `--tags` (optional, multiple) - Space-separated tags, default empty list
- `--notes` (optional) - Notes string, default empty string

**Output:** Prints bookmark ID on success

---

#### Command: `list`
**Location:** `src/cli.py:61-66`
```python
elif args.command == "list":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(bookmarks))
    else:
        print(format_table(bookmarks))
```
**Arguments:**
- `--format` (choices: `table`, `json`) - Output format, default `table`

**Output:** Formatted list of all bookmarks

---

#### Command: `delete`
**Location:** `src/cli.py:68-74`
```python
elif args.command == "delete":
    success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
    if success:
        print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
    else:
        print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
        sys.exit(1)
```
**Arguments:**
- `id` (required) - UUID of bookmark to delete

**Output:** Success/failure message. Exits with code 1 if not found.

---

#### Command: `show`
**Location:** `src/cli.py:76-81`
```python
elif args.command == "show":
    bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
    if bookmark is None:
        print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
        sys.exit(1)
    print(format_table([bookmark]))
```
**Arguments:**
- `id` (required) - UUID of bookmark to display

**Output:** Single bookmark formatted as table. Exits with code 1 if not found.

---

### 4. OUTPUT FORMATTING PATTERNS

**Location:** `/absolute/path/src/formatter.py`

#### Table Format
**Function:** `format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`

**Layout:**
- ID (8 chars) | Title (30 chars) | URL (40 chars) | Tags (20 chars)
- Fixed-width columns with left-alignment
- Header row + separator line + data rows
- Text truncated to column width if too long
- Tags joined with ", "
- Shows "No bookmarks found." if empty list

**Example Output:**
```
ID       Title                          URL                                      Tags                
-------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------- --------------------
aaa-111  Example Site                   https://example.com                      example, test       
bbb-222  Python Docs                    https://docs.python.org/3/               python, docs        
```

#### JSON Format
**Function:** `format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`

**Output:**
- Direct JSON serialization of `[b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]`
- Pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
- All fields included: id, url, title, tags, notes, created_at

---

### 5. DATA MODEL

**Location:** `/absolute/path/src/models.py`

**Class:** `Bookmark` (dataclass)

**Fields:**
```python
id: str                          # UUID (auto-generated on creation)
url: str                         # Full URL string
title: str                       # Bookmark title
tags: List[str]                  # List of tag strings (default: [])
notes: str                       # Notes/description (default: "")
created_at: str                  # ISO format datetime (auto-generated)
```

**Key Methods:**

**`to_dict() -> dict`**
- Converts bookmark to dictionary representation
- Returns all 6 fields as dict keys

**`from_dict(data: dict) -> Bookmark` (classmethod)**
- Deserializes dict to Bookmark object
- Handles missing optional fields with defaults:
  - `tags` defaults to `[]`
  - `notes` defaults to `""`
  - `created_at` defaults to current ISO datetime if missing

---

### 6. DATA STORAGE & PERSISTENCE

**Location:** `/absolute/path/src/storage.py`

**Storage Method:** JSON file-based (not a database)

**Default Path:** `~/.bookmarks.json` (user's home directory)

#### Core Functions:

**`load_bookmarks(db_path=DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Reads JSON file and deserializes all bookmarks
- Returns empty list if file doesn't exist
- Uses `Bookmark.from_dict()` for deserialization

**`save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path=DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None`**
- Serializes all bookmarks to JSON using `to_dict()`
- Creates file if doesn't exist
- Writes with 2-space indentation
- Overwrites entire file (full replacement pattern)

**`add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path=DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Bookmark`**
- Generates UUID using `uuid.uuid4()`
- Creates Bookmark object with auto-generated timestamp
- Loads existing bookmarks, appends new one, saves all
- Returns the created bookmark object

**`delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path=DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool`**
- Loads all bookmarks
- Filters out matching ID using list comprehension
- Only saves if something was actually deleted
- Returns True if deleted, False if ID not found

**`get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path=DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]`**
- Loads all bookmarks
- Linear search for matching ID
- Returns bookmark or None if not found

### 7. FILTERING & QUERY PATTERNS

**IMPORTANT:** Search functionality is **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED** in the codebase, but tests define the expected behavior.

**`search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**

**`search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Exact tag matching (case-sensitive)
- Returns all bookmarks with that tag
- Example: "docs" tag returns 3 bookmarks

**`search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Matches URL domain (including subdomains)
- Example: "github.com" matches both "github.com/runpod/..." and "github.com/pallets/..."
- Example: "docs.python.org" matches "docs.python.org" subdomains

**`search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Combined search with AND logic (all specified criteria must match)
- If no criteria provided, returns all bookmarks
- If criteria conflict, returns empty list
- Examples:
  - `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")` returns only bookmarks with "python" in title AND "sdk" tag
  - `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")` uses AND logic across all three

### 9. KEY PATTERNS & CONVENTIONS

**ID Generation:** UUID v4 strings (36 chars including hyphens)

**Date/Time:** ISO format strings (e.g., "2026-01-01T00:00:00")

**Configuration:** Single HOME_DIR file location, configurable in function calls

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add `search` command to bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI tool (`src/cli.py`) supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no search functionality. A full test suite already exists at `tests/test_search.py` (180 lines) that defines the exact expected behavior and imports from a not-yet-created `src/search.py` module. The goal is to implement the search module and wire it into the CLI so all tests pass.

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create a new module with four public functions. The tests import them as:
```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

### `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **Case-insensitive substring match** on `bookmark.title`
- Implementation: `query.lower() in b.title.lower()`
- Tests: "python" matches "Python Tutorial" and "RunPod Python SDK"

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **Exact match** against each bookmark's `tags` list
- Implementation: `tag in b.tags`
- Tests: "flask" matches 1, "docs" matches 3, "rust" matches 0

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Match the **hostname** portion of the URL against the given domain
- Use `urllib.parse.urlparse(b.url).netloc` to extract the hostname
- Check if netloc equals or ends with the domain: `netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain)`
- Tests: "github.com" matches 2 URLs (`github.com/...`), "docs.python.org" matches 2 URLs (`docs.python.org/...`)

### `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **AND logic**: apply each provided criterion sequentially, filtering the list
- If no criteria given, return all bookmarks
- Implementation: start with full list, then filter through each non-None criterion using the helper functions above
- Tests: `search(bm, title="python", tag="sdk")` returns 1 (AND); `search(bm)` returns all 5

## Step 2: Add `search` subcommand to CLI parser in `src/cli.py`

In `build_parser()`, add after the `show` subparser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format", choices=["table", "json"], default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

Tests verify: `parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])` yields `args.command == "search"` and `args.title == "python"`.

## Step 3: Add `search` command handler in `main()` in `src/cli.py`

Add an `elif` branch for the search command, following the same pattern as the `list` command:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

Add the import at the top of `cli.py`:
```python
from .search import search
```

## Files to modify
- **Create:** `src/search.py` (new ~35-line module)
- **Modify:** `src/cli.py` (add import, parser subcommand, and handler — ~15 lines added)

## Verification

Run the full test suite:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 24 tests should pass (11 existing in `test_storage.py` + 13 new in `test_search.py`).

# Plan: Add `search` command to bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI tool (`src/cli.py`) supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no search functionality. A full test suite already exists at `tests/test_search.py` (180 lines) that defines the exact expected behavior and imports from a not-yet-created `src/search.py` module. The goal is to implement the search module and wire it into the CLI so all tests pass.

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create a new module with four public functions. The tests import them as:
```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

### `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **Case-insensitive substring match** on `bookmark.title`
- Implementation: `query.lower() in b.title.lower()`
- Tests: "python" matches "Python Tutorial" and "RunPod Python SDK"

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **Exact match** against each bookmark's `tags` list
- Implementation: `tag in b.tags`
- Tests: "flask" matches 1, "docs" matches 3, "rust" matches 0

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Match the **hostname** portion of the URL against the given domain
- Use `urllib.parse.urlparse(b.url).netloc` to extract the hostname
- Check if netloc equals or ends with the domain: `netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain)`
- Tests: "github.com" matches 2 URLs (`github.com/...`), "docs.python.org" matches 2 URLs (`docs.python.org/...`)

### `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- **AND logic**: apply each provided criterion sequentially, filtering the list
- If no criteria given, return all bookmarks
- Implementation: start with full list, then filter through each non-None criterion using the helper functions above
- Tests: `search(bm, title="python", tag="sdk")` returns 1 (AND); `search(bm)` returns all 5

## Step 2: Add `search` subcommand to CLI parser in `src/cli.py`

In `build_parser()`, add after the `show` subparser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")

search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")

search_parser.add_argument(

    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

Tests verify: `parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])` yields `args.command == "search"` and `args.title == "python"`.

```python
elif args.command == "search":
```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.storage import (
    add_bookmark,
    delete_bookmark,
    get_bookmark,
    load_bookmarks,
    save_bookmarks,
)


@pytest.fixture
def db_path(tmp_path):
    return str(tmp_path / "test_bookmarks.json")


@pytest.fixture
def sample_bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://example.com",
            title="Example Site",
            tags=["example", "test"],
            notes="A sample bookmark",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/",
            title="Python Docs",
            tags=["python", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python documentation",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


class TestLoadBookmarks:
    def test_returns_empty_list_when_file_missing(self, db_path):
        result = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert result == []

    def test_loads_bookmarks_from_file(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(result) == 2
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"
        assert result[1].title == "Python Docs"


class TestSaveBookmarks:
    def test_creates_file(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        assert os.path.exists(db_path)

    def test_file_contains_valid_json(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        with open(db_path) as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        assert len(data) == 2
        assert data[0]["url"] == "https://example.com"


class TestAddBookmark:
    def test_adds_bookmark_and_returns_it(self, db_path):
        result = add_bookmark(
            url="https://new.example.com",
            title="New Bookmark",
            tags=["new"],
            db_path=db_path,
        )
        assert result.url == "https://new.example.com"
        assert result.title == "New Bookmark"

    def test_persists_bookmark(self, db_path):
        add_bookmark(url="https://a.com", title="A", db_path=db_path)
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(bookmarks) == 1

    def test_generates_unique_ids(self, db_path):
        b1 = add_bookmark(url="https://a.com", title="A", db_path=db_path)
        b2 = add_bookmark(url="https://b.com", title="B", db_path=db_path)
        assert b1.id != b2.id


class TestDeleteBookmark:
    def test_deletes_existing_bookmark(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = delete_bookmark("aaa-111", db_path)
        assert result is True
        remaining = load_bookmarks(db_path)
        assert len(remaining) == 1

    def test_returns_false_for_missing_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = delete_bookmark("nonexistent", db_path)
        assert result is False


class TestGetBookmark:
    def test_returns_bookmark_by_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = get_bookmark("bbb-222", db_path)
        assert result is not None
        assert result.title == "Python Docs"

    def test_returns_none_for_missing_id(self, db_path, sample_bookmarks):
        save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
        result = get_bookmark("nonexistent", db_path)
        assert result is None

"""Search functionality for filtering bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains the given substring (case-insensitive)."""
    title_lower = title.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if title_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for the given tag."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL domain matches the given domain."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Filter bookmarks using AND logic across all provided criteria.

    Any criterion that is None is skipped. When no criteria are provided,
    all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
